recommendations.text: Add a model/param preflight validation at compile or activation time: (1) reject or warn when an engine config pins a model that does not exist at the provider endpoint (e.g. codex 'adelie-alpha-2026-02-19' -> 404); (2) reject or strip params the chosen model does not support (e.g. 'effort'/reasoning-effort on a claude model that returns 400 'does not support the effort parameter'). These currently waste an entire prod-main scheduled run (all retries exhausted, 0 tokens) on what is a static config error. Audit Daily Caveman Optimizer and Daily Cache Strategy Analyzer frontmatter for stale model ids/params.

Add a soft pre-cap guard for heavy daily-aggregation workflows (Daily Ambient Context Optimizer ran 25.37M eff this window, OVER the 25M cap, and only narrowly succeeded): chunk/stream the context or set a 22-23M effective-token ceiling that triggers graceful summarization before hitting the hard 25M 429. #35661.

Make the safe_outputs job tolerant of per-item failures: when the agent succeeded and >=1 item landed, exit non-red with a partial-success summary instead of failing the whole job on a single rejected item. Quiet this window but structurally unresolved.
For add_comment with target='*', require/inject item_number at emit-time or give the agent a clearer schema hint. Not triggered 2026-06-05 but fix not yet shipped.

Investigate copilot-sdk-driver tool-permission lockout: fail fast with a clear error instead of looping until the 600s session.idle timeout. Quiet 2026-06-05 (PR Code Quality Reviewer 2/2 success) but keep the startup self-check on the roadmap.

Investigate why Test Quality Sentinel [copilot] reddened the agent job despite producing a complete valid agent_output report (27 turns, no crash, error_count=0). Capture full harness stdio (the log appeared truncated to report markdown) to isolate the nonzero exit. Distinguish from the copilot-sdk-driver class.
Add label + temporary_id support to the create_discussion safe-output surface (re-requested by Smoke Copilot probe 2026-06-05).
